blocks.description: Animal Walks: Bear walk, frog jumps, crab walk across the room. End with 5 wall pushes.
Letter Hunt: Hide 5 magnetic letters (L, U, C, A, M) around room. Find them, name them, jump to a tape letter match on floor.
Free movement: Dance to one favorite song or play with blocks.
Picture Description: Use 3 familiar picture cards (animal, vehicle, food). Child says simple sentence: 'I see a ___' or 'The ___ is ___.' Parent models first.
Letter L Painting: Paint large letter L on paper. Decorate with stickers or fingerprints.
Clean up art supplies together. Sing cleanup song. High five and praise effort.
Balloon Tap or Ball Toss: Keep balloon in air 10 times, or toss ball back and forth while counting.
Counting Jump Course: Parent calls out number 1-5. Child jumps that many times. Then count blocks together to match.
Snack time: crackers and water. Child can count crackers before eating.
Play-Doh Letters: Roll Play-Doh snakes and form letters L, U, C, A. Squeeze and roll for hand strength.
Alphabet Song with Actions: Sing ABC song. Jump or clap on letters in name (L-U-C-A).
Put Play-Doh away. Wipe table together. Celebrate: 'You did great learning today!'
Obstacle Course: Crawl under chair, jump over pillow, run to wall and back.
Rhyme Time Game: Parent says word (cat), child finds picture that rhymes (hat, bat). Use 3-4 picture pairs. Move to each match.
Free play with Legos or blocks.
Two-Step Directions with Visual: 'Get the ball AND put it in the basket.' 'Touch the red block AND give it to me.' 3-4 tries with objects.
Shape Stamping: Use sponges or cookie cutters. Stamp circle, square, triangle on paper. Name shapes together.
Cleanup art. Wash hands. High five. 'You listened so well today!'
Freeze Dance: Dance to music, freeze when music stops. 3-4 rounds.
Shape Hunt: Find circle, square, triangle items around the house. Sort them by shape.
Quiet break: look at a favorite book or play with toy cars.
Tweezers and Pom-Poms: Use tweezers or tongs to move 10 pom-poms from bowl to bowl. Then sort by color.
Rhythm Clapping: Clap to syllables in words (Li-on, el-e-phant, car). Use animal names. Make it a game.
Put materials away. Celebrate: 'You were a great learner!'
Simon Says Body Parts: Touch your nose, jump 3 times, touch your toes, spin around.
Beginning Sound Sort: Parent says sound /m/. Child finds 3 objects that start with /m/ (marker, mouse toy, mat). Repeat with /b/, /s/.
Heavy work: carry stack of books or push laundry basket across room.
Story Sequence: Use 3 pictures (first, next, last). Tell simple story together. Parent models, child repeats.
Sticker Pattern: Make pattern with stickers (red-blue-red-blue). Then let child make own pattern.
Cleanup together. Sing cleanup song. Praise: 'You did great work!'
Outdoor play: Kick ball back and forth, run to tree and back. (Weather is sunny!)
Block Patterns: Build pattern with blocks (red-blue-red-blue). Child continues pattern. Try 2-3 different patterns.
Snack time with counting: Count grapes or crackers before eating.
Cutting Practice: Cut Play-Doh snakes or cut paper strips (5 cuts). Use child-safe scissors.
Chalk Drawing Outside: Draw shapes, letters, or free draw with chalk. Large movements.
Wash hands. Put materials away. High five. 'Great job today!'
Number Jump: Parent holds up number card 1-5. Child jumps that many times. 5 rounds.
Number Hunt: Hide number cards 1-10 around room. Find them in order. Count objects to match each number.
Free play: Legos, puzzles, or quiet toy.
Tell-Me Story: Use toy animals. Child makes simple 3-part story (first animal comes, next animal plays, last animal goes home). Parent scaffolds.
Number Song: Sing counting songs (5 Little Ducks, 5 Little Monkeys). Use fingers or toys.
Put toys away. Celebrate: 'You worked so hard!'
Animal Yoga: Cat stretch, dog pose, frog squat, butterfly wings. Hold each 10 seconds.
Letter Bingo: Use bingo board with 6 letters (L, U, C, A, M, B). Parent calls letter, child covers it. Play 2 rounds.
Quiet time: look at picture book or play with sensory bin (rice, small toys).
Name Building: Build name LUCA with magnetic letters, then trace each letter in sand tray or shaving cream.
Number Painting: Paint numbers 1-5 on paper. Decorate with dots matching the number.
Cleanup. Wash hands. High five. 'You did amazing learning!'
Parade March: March around house with music. Carry rhythm sticks or shake shakers.
Letter Review Game: Parent hides 5 letters learned this week. Child finds them and names them. Jump to match on floor.
Choice time: play with favorite toy.
I Spy Speech: I spy with my little eye something that starts with /b/. Child guesses (ball, book, banana). Parent and child take turns.
Free Art Friday: Child chooses: paint, draw, Play-Doh, or stickers. Minimal instruction. Just create.
Cleanup. Sing cleanup song. Celebrate week: 'You learned so much this week!'
Outdoor Free Play: Run, kick ball, or play tag. (Sunny weather!)
Counting Treasure Hunt: Hide 10 small toys. Child finds them, counts them, sorts them by type or color.
Snack + story: Eat snack while parent reads 1-2 pages of favorite book.
Clothespin Game: Clip 10 clothespins around edge of bowl. Child removes them. Then clip them onto cardstock letters.
Dance Party: Play 2 favorite songs. Dance freely. Celebrate the week!
Quick cleanup. Big celebration: 'You are a great learner! Have a wonderful weekend!'
